contentBlocks.original:
ईश्वरो गुरुरात्मेति मूर्तिभेदविभागिने |
व्योमवद् व्याप्तदेहाय दक्षिणामूर्तये नमः ‖
contentBlocks.transliteration:
īśvaro gururātmeti mūrtibheda vibhāgine |
vyomavad vyāptadehāya dakṣiṇāmūrtaye namaḥ ‖
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to Lord Dakshinamurti, who is all-pervasive like space but who appears (as though) divided as Lord, Guru, and the Self.
contentBlocks.meanings: ईश्वरः (īśvaraḥ) – Lord; गुरुः (guruḥ) – Guru; आत्मा (ātmā) – Self; इति (iti) – thus; मूर्तिभेदविभागिने (mūrtibhedavibhāgine) – to the one who appears divided (mūrti:form | bheda:division | vibhāgine:to the one who appears); व्योमवत् (vyomavat) – like space; व्याप्तदेहाय (vyāptadehāya) – to the all-pervasive one (vyāpta:pervasive | dehāya:to the one with a body); दक्षिणामूर्तये (dakṣiṇāmūrtaye) – to Dakshinamurti; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations;
